reasoning: The Federal Reserve's Bank Term Funding Program (BTFP) stopped extending new loans on March 11, 2024. Because loans were limited to a one-year maximum term, all existing BTFP loans matured and were repaid by March 11, 2025. This is confirmed by the FRED time series, which shows the outstanding balance dropping exactly to 0 on March 12, 2025, and remaining at zero ever since. For the program balance to increase by either resolution date, the Fed would need to unexpectedly revive this specific COVID-era/SVB-era emergency lending facility. Barring a tremendous and sudden banking crisis within the next month, this defunct program will almost definitively maintain a zero balance. I estimate the probability of any increase to be at the minimum allowed bounds.
